title: Jayson Tatum explains what’s allowed Jaylen Brown to turn into an MVP contender
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown averages career-highs in points, rebounds, and assists, emerging as a viable MVP contender this season.
Jayson Tatum credits Brown's increased opportunities and leadership for his progress in becoming Boston's go-to scorer.
The Celtics defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder 119-109, solidifying their status as a championship contender this spring.

title: In a fitting turn, Casey Mittelstadt delivered in one of Bruins’ gutsiest wins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Casey Mittelstadt scored his 14th goal of the season to tie the game, helping the Bruins win 4-3 in Buffalo.
The win improved Boston Bruins' playoff position to 88 points in 72 games, ahead of key rivals in the standings.
Coach Marco Sturm credits Mittelstadt's defensive improvement, contributing to his key role on Boston's second line.

title: Why is Jaylen Brown getting more trips to the free-throw line? ‘Maybe complaining’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown's complaints about officiating may be influencing an increase in his free-throw opportunities.
Brown scored 31 points with 12 of 14 free throws in Celtics' win against OKC Thunder.
Celtics and OKC had nearly equal free-throw attempts; Celtics shot 29, Thunder shot 26.

title: How did James Hagens fare in his pro debut with the Providence Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens made his pro debut with the Providence Bruins but recorded no points in their 2-1 victory.
Hagens, playing on the top line, showcased his skating on the power play despite Providence going 0-for-4.
The 19-year-old faced challenges in physical play but showed potential in transition and offensive zone faceoffs.

title: Jaylen Brown leads Celtics to statement win over Thunder: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jaylen Brown scored 31 points, leading the Celtics to a 119-109 victory over the Thunder, ending their 12-game winning streak.
Brown and Jayson Tatum combined for 15 assists as they exploited defensive gaps in OKC's strategy.
Baylor Scheierman continues to impress, scoring 11 points and defending strongly in 20 minutes of play.

title: 2 Boston men accused of assaulting man who hit their car while trying to park
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Anthony Shearrion and Arkie Swinson are charged with assault after allegedly beating a man in Dorchester.
The incident occurred after the victim accidentally hit their parked vehicle on Blue Hill Avenue, according to police.
The suspects are held at Suffolk County Jail and have a court date set for April 14.

title: 2026 Red Sox preview: 4 questions facing Alex Cora’s club
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Red Sox face lineup, bullpen, and defensive challenges as they enter the 2026 season.
Roman Anthony leads a young core aiming to boost the Red Sox's offensive performance this year.
Manager Alex Cora must resolve an outfield/DH logjam with players like Jarren Duran expected to transition roles.

title: Healey announces kickoff of public safety initiative for 2026 World Cup
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Governor Healey announced the 'Match-Ready Massachusetts' safety plan for the 2026 World Cup, securing $76 million in federal funds.
Foxborough's Gillette Stadium, renamed Boston Stadium for the World Cup, will host seven matches from June 13 to July 9.
Special MBTA schedules and four event trains will facilitate transportation for the World Cup and other large events in Boston.

title: Kraft-owned New England Revolution backing White Stadium renovation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The New England Revolution supports the renovation of White Stadium, a project led by Boston Mayor Michelle Wu.
White Stadium's renovation, costing $135 million, faces legal challenges from the Franklin Park Defenders group.
Boston Legacy FC will invest $190 million and pay over $252 million in community benefits over 15 years.

title: Maine man in critical condition after stopping to help stranded driver
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Maine man critically injured on I-95 in Veazie after aiding a stranded driver during snowy conditions.
A 54-year-old stopped to help an 18-year-old woman from Bangor when a car lost control and struck them.
Police say the man may have attempted to shield the woman; he is in critical condition, both drivers sustained minor injuries.
